'''Willie Peters''' (born 1 March 1979) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. 

Peters played in the 1990s and 2000s for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] as a {{rlp|hb}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/city-east/former-souths-player-willie-peters-brings-elite-sport-skills-to-maroubra/news-story/231645a0f3c533e17f14cc8210ea68a1|title=NRL training for teens|first=Laura|last=Suckling|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=9 October 2013}}</ref> He had also previously played for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] as well as in England for [[Gateshead Thunder (2000)|Gateshead]], [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] and [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].

==Background==
Willie Peters was born in [[Sydney]], Australia and is of [[Greeks|Greek]] heritage.

==Playing career==
While playing for Souths in the 1990s, coach [[Craig Coleman]] declared that Peters could be the next [[Peter Sterling (rugby league commentator)|Peter Sterling]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/souths-great-says-rabbitohs-halfback-adam-reynolds-can-go-far-in-rugby-league/news-story/c17bfc48be7cd4d613bdc957e007c370 |title=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=15 September 2017 |archive-date=11 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200111024506/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/souths-great-says-rabbitohs-halfback-adam-reynolds-can-go-far-in-rugby-league/news-story/c17bfc48be7cd4d613bdc957e007c370 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
In 1999, Peters joined Gateshead before joining Wigan in 2000.  Peters played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] at scrum half back in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]

In 2001, Peters joined [[St. George Illawarra|St. George]] playing with the club for two seasons before returning to Souths in 2003.  Peters played his two final seasons in Australia with South Sydney where the club finished with back to back wooden spoons.  In 2004, Peters joined Widnes and played one season with the club before retiring.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_p/player_peters_willie.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report – Willie Peters|website=Ssralmanac.com}}</ref>

==Post playing==
In 2016, Peters became head coach of the [[Wests Tigers]] [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup]] team. In 2017, Peters became the assistant coach at [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]. On 15 September 2017, Peters left his position at Manly after it was alleged he had been involved in a fight with another Manly staff member at a [[public house|pub]] in [[The Rocks, New South Wales|The Rocks]], Sydney.  Peters was issued with an infringement notice by police for offensive behaviour. The Manly club later released a statement which read "The Sea Eagles will not tolerate misconduct and expect all employees to represent the club at the highest standard," Manly owner and chairman Scott Penn said. "All employees agree to the club's code of conduct and know what is expected of them. We must uphold these standards and will not accept any avoidable breach of conduct".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2017-manly-sea-eagles-assistant-coach-willie-peters-leaves-club-after-pub-fight-with-colleague-20170915-gyibqb.html/|title=Manly Sea Eagles assistant coach Willie Peters leaves club after pub fight with colleague|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=15 September 2017 }}</ref>

After leaving Manly, Peters became an NRL assistant coach under [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] at the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in 2019,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/sacked-manly-sea-eagles-assistant-coach-willie-peters-joins-south-sydney-rabbitohs/1ffsoa3cv0le41u1w4mpx62yyy/|title=Sacked Manly coach Willie Peters joins Souths|website=Sporting News|date=11 October 2017 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/sea-eagles/nrl-2017-manly-assistant-coach-willie-peters-sacked-after-pub-fist-fight/news-story/71b0338a6c937a9f245d243e3c4ed0a2/|title=Manly assistant coach Willie Peters sacked after pub fist fight|work=Fox Sports|date=15 September 2017 }}</ref> before joining with the Newcastle Knights in 2020 as assistant coach.

In May 2022, Peters was named as the new coach of [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]], succeeding [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]]. Peters took charge at the end of the [[Super League XXVII|2022 season]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Willie Peters to take over from Tony Smith as Hull KR coach on three-year deal from 2023 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12217/12609323/willie-peters-to-take-over-from-tony-smith-as-hull-kr-coach-on-three-year-deal-from-2023 |access-date=9 May 2022 |publisher=Sky Sports |date=9 May 2022}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Peters guided  Rovers to the [[2023 Challenge Cup Final]] against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]], however, the club would lose 17-16 after golden point extra-time.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport |date=12 August 2023 }}</ref>
Peters later guided Hull KR to fourth place, and qualification for the playoffs, where they reached the semi-final, but were defeated 42-12 against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

In 2024, Peters guided Hull Kingston Rovers to the [[2024 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]], after narrowly defeating [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] 10–8 in the semi finals. On 8 October 2024, Peters was named as the Super League coach of the year. On 12 October 2024, Peters coached Hull Kingston Rovers in their [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|newspaper=The Guardian|date=12 October 2024 |last1=Calvert |first1=Lee }}</ref>

On 7 June 2025, Peters coached Hull Kingston Rovers to their 8-6 2025 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In October 2025, Peter became assistant head coach of {{rlnt|AUS}} in addition to his role with KR.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/willie-peters-first-words-as-australia-appointment-for-ashes-confirmed|title=Willie Peters’ first words as Australia appointment for Ashes confirmed|date=6 October 2025|publisher=Love Rugby League}}</ref>
In the same month, Peters won the 2025 Super League coach of the year award.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/willie-peters-beast-mode-message-10558847/|title=Willie Peters’ ‘beast mode’ message to Hull KR’s Mikey Lewis with NRL Grand Final call|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

On 11 October 2025, Peters coached Hull Kingston Rovers in their 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In December 2025, Peters was awarded an Honorary Doctor of Letters (DLitt) by the University of Hull in recognition of his leadership and contribution to rugby league, coinciding with the premiere of Hull KR’s documentary series ''Renaissance'', which documented the club’s treble-winning season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nz.news.yahoo.com/hull-kr-head-coach-receives-184711519.html?guccounter=1&guce_referrer=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ29vZ2xlLmNvbS8&guce_referrer_sig=AQAAAGGFJv3bZwXmwYvBp_pUhCwQN8xTtMy8YvqVzmEk3Bqt9ixX8L_Yv1XAS4i8iVU_3EDnxVwFS41NhYrdmhGMrnZm2Ic-ClpAF2nm9jaNU97PseN_dkdrXdDa6OZ1C19J5veXfMSCanQxEJvEzGOOnJpmLzMvJ_UZbZ0ji16ks868|title=Hull KR head coach receives honorary degree|website=www.nz.news.yahoo.com}}</ref>

On 19 February 2026, Peters guided Hull Kingston Rovers to their [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In March 2026, Peters announced the 2026 season would be his last with Hull KR, in addition to stepping down as Australia assistant coach, with Peters taking the head coach role at the new NRL franchise [[PNG Chiefs]] for 2027.<ref>https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/willie-peters-makes-decision-on-england-job-as-rfl-forced-into-action</ref>
